title: Bridge Essay: Auditory verbal hallucinations: The ins and outs of subtypes.
concept: Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

**Auditory verbal hallucinations: The ins and outs of subtypes.** — Psychiatry Res (2025). DOI: 10.1016/j.psychres.2025.116696. PMID: 40840195. BACKGROUND: Voice-hearing in psychosis is a heterogeneous and often distressing experience. Phenomenological studies have proposed the existence of several subtypes of auditory verbal hallucinations (AVHs) that may guide intervention, such as inner-speech based voices (ISVs), hypervigilance voices (HVs) and memory-based voices, but empirical data is scarce. We developed a detailed coding scheme with the aim of examining whether distinct subtypes of AVHs could be reliably identified and character
